$comment: ELF64 EXECUTABLE (ET_EXEC) for x86_64 Linux. Second ELF schema (plan 14 LK1 cycle 2, 2026-05-30). Consumed by `src/link/format/elf.cpp` via the format-blind linker engine. Differs from `elf64-x86_64-linux.format.json` (the .o schema) in: `elf.type = exec`, sections carry `virtualAddress` (used as sh_addr), top-level `entryPoint` names the entry symbol (resolved against AssembledModule at link time). Cycle-2 scope: self-contained modules (zero relocations). Modules with relocations fail loud — the cross-reference applier lives at LK6 (D-LK4-5 + D-LK4-6 + D-LK1-3).
$dataModelComment: FC3 c1: 64-bit linux/darwin are LP64 (int 32, long 64, ptr 64). Consumed by the semantic tier (buildCuMir threads format.dataModel() into analyze()): c-subset long binds I64 here. REQUIRED closed-enum field; the loader rejects a missing or unknown value (a silent default would bake wrong widths).
$bitFieldStrategyComment: D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ABI-EXACT: SysV/GNU LSB-first bit-field packing (byte-ABI-exact to gcc). OS/format-determined (x86_64 serves both ELF and PE), so on the format not the target. See elf64-x86_64-linux for the full rule.
$entryPointComment: Empty entryPoint defaults the walker to module.functions[0] (cycle-2 convention). Once D-LK1-1 closes (real symbol-name thread through HIR→LIR→AssembledFunction), this field will name the entry symbol (e.g. '_start' for Linux/glibc, 'main' for hand-rolled execs) and the walker will resolve it by name. When D-LK10-ENTRY Slice C lands, the linker prepends a synthetic `_start` trampoline as module.functions[0] and the schema's entryPoint names the USER fn (the trampoline's call target).
$externCallDispatchComment: ELF resolves imports through the PLT: the linker synthesizes a PLT stub per extern (jmp through the GOT slot) and points the extern symbol's VA at the STUB, so the call site is a PLAIN DIRECT call (E8 disp32 = call rel32 to the stub). An indirect-slot (FF 15) call site would dereference the stub's CODE bytes as a pointer -> SIGSEGV. D-FFI-EXTERN-CALL-DISPATCH.
$dataImportBindingComment: c84 (D-LK-EXTERN-DATA-IMPORT): extern library DATA objects (libc stdout/stderr/stdin) bind via ELF COPY RELOCATIONS — the standard glibc non-PIE ET_EXEC mechanism: the exec reserves a correctly-sized+aligned .bss slot per imported object, exports the symbol as a DEFINED OBJECT (st_size = the layout-derived object size) at that slot, and emits one R_X86_64_COPY (5) in .rela.dyn; ld-linux memcpy's the library's object into the slot before entry and ALL images (incl. libc itself, by interposition) reference the exec's copy. Zero new instruction encodings — module code reaches the object through the normal GlobalAddr path. A format WITHOUT this field rejects data imports loud at the linker's pre-walker gate (a data symbol bound through the function-import machinery would read jump-stub bytes as the object's value). The PIE-forward GOT-indirect model is a future member.
$supportedDataSectionsComment: D-LK1-ELF-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS (rodata) + D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER (writable `.data` + zero-fill `.bss`): the ELF ET_EXEC walker emits `.rodata` (read-only, R+X PT_LOAD), `.data` (mutable initialized globals, R+W PT_LOAD), and `.bss` (zero-init globals, R+W PT_LOAD, NOBITS) from `AssembledModule.dataItems` per the item's `section`. The linker's pre-walker gate (linker.cpp) consults this set per-item. Cross-format agnosticism: this is the SAME field PE/Mach-O exec formats declare; the gate has zero format-name branches.
$artifactProfilesComment: Profiles this format SERVES (plan 06 AP3) — a runnable console executable ⇒ cli. The format-side twin of the language's artifactProfiles[] (AP1): the driver fails loud (D_ArtifactProfileFormatMismatch) if a project's profile isn't in this set. A future shared-library format would declare ["lib"] with ZERO gate-code change — the gate is a generic set-membership, never a format-name branch.
$processExitComment: STDIO-FLUSH-AT-EXIT (2026-06-09): terminate via libc `exit(3)`, NOT the raw `exit_group(2)` syscall. The C standard says returning from `main` ≡ calling `exit(3)`, which flushes line-/full-buffered stdio + runs `atexit` handlers before the process dies. The prior raw `exit_group` syscall skipped that flush, so a program that did `puts("hello")` then returned exited cleanly but with stdout still buffered (empty on a pipe). By-name-import of libc `exit` makes the trampoline emit a DIRECT `call` to the PLT stub for `exit` (per externCallDispatch=direct-plt), exactly as a user-level FFI call (`puts`) lowers — so `exit` flushes stdout, runs atexit, then calls `_exit`/`exit_group` itself. This routes EVERY ELF program through the dynamic writer (libc.so.6 becomes a DT_NEEDED, a PLT stub + GOT slot is synthesized for `exit`, ld-linux resolves it) — the SAME machinery `extern_call_elf` already uses; a program whose ONLY extern is this `exit` works because it is identical in shape to the FFI case. importMangledName is the canonical C name `exit` (ELF adds no leading underscore — cFormatAddsLeadingUnderscore(Elf)=false). Uniform with macho (libSystem `exit`) + pe (kernel32 `ExitProcess`, which flushes via msvcrt's DLL-detach handler): all three terminate through a libc-level, stdio-flushing primitive.
$entryCallingConventionComment: SysV AMD64 calling convention — status (user fn return value in rax) is moved into argGprs[0] = rdi before SYSCALL. Read via `target.callingConventionByName("sysv_amd64")` at trampoline-build time.
$processArgsComment: c88 (D-RUNTIME-MAIN-ARGC-ARGV): program-entry argc/argv materialization. SysV AMD64 psABI §3.4.1 — at the process entry point the kernel/loader leaves argc (a machine word) at [rsp+0] and the NULL-terminated argv pointer vector IN PLACE starting at [rsp+8] (envp follows argv's NULL; not materialized — the c-subset entry signature is (int, char**)). The trampoline emits `mov rdi, [rsp+0]` (argc → argGprs[0]) + `lea rsi, [rsp+8]` (the vector's ADDRESS → argGprs[1]) BEFORE any SP adjustment and before `call main`. A main(void) simply ignores the two registers (C-legal) — signature-independent setup. Without this block, main(argc,argv) reads process-entry register garbage (the c87 sqlite3 shell crash: argc=846361312). Destination registers come from entryCallingConvention.argGprs[0..1] — never declared here (single source of truth).

elf.$comment: Elf64_Ehdr identity for an executable. type = ET_EXEC (2). class = ELFCLASS64; data = ELFDATA2LSB; osabi = sysv; machine = EM_X86_64 (62). pageAlign = 4096 (Linux x86_64 4 KB pages — kernel's PT_LOAD congruence requires p_vaddr % p_align == p_offset % p_align). ARM64 Linux on Apple Silicon / 64K configurations declares 16384 / 65536 in its own schema (D-LK6-3).
elf.$bindNowComment: Eager binding — the v1 stance per plan 14 §5 risk row. Emits DT_FLAGS_1 = DF_1_NOW so dyld resolves every GOT slot at load via R_X86_64_GLOB_DAT in .rela.dyn. Lazy upgrade (.rela.plt + R_X86_64_JUMP_SLOT + 16-byte PLT0 resolver-trampoline) anchored at D-LK6-11; until then, the walker fails loud on bindNow=false.

sections.$comment: Executable code section. virtualAddress = 0x401000 — conventional Linux x86_64 base (0x400000) + 0x1000 to leave the first page for the Ehdr and program header table loaded alongside `.text` in the same PT_LOAD. This is a convention, not a kernel hard requirement: any page-aligned VA works as long as `p_vaddr % p_align == p_offset % p_align`. sh_addr is set to this; e_entry derives from this + entry-function offset. sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC | SHF_EXECINSTR (6); sh_addralign = 16 (x86_64 psABI).
sections.name: .text

sections.$comment: Read-only initialized data (string literals, const/primitive globals). sh_type=SHT_PROGBITS(1); sh_flags=SHF_ALLOC(2) — readable, NOT writable/executable (it is folded into the R+X text PT_LOAD this cycle, which is strictly less permissive than writable; W^X preserved). sh_addralign=8. virtualAddress=0 — the writer COMPUTES the rodata VA contiguously after .text (secText.virtualAddress + alignUp(text.size, 8)); a fixed VA would collide with .text. D-LK1-ELF-EXEC-DATA-SECTIONS.
sections.name: .rodata

sections.$comment: Writable initialized data (mutable primitive/aggregate globals). sh_type=SHT_PROGBITS(1); sh_flags=SHF_ALLOC|SHF_WRITE(3) — readable + WRITABLE (a store into it must not fault). Placed in a SEPARATE R+W PT_LOAD from the R+X text/rodata so mutable data never shares a page with code (W^X). sh_addralign=8. virtualAddress=0 — the writer COMPUTES the `.data` VA (page-aligned writable segment after the read-only sections). D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER.
sections.name: .data

sections.$comment: Zero-initialized data (tentative globals `int g;`). sh_type=SHT_NOBITS(8) — occupies NO file bytes; the loader zero-fills sh_size bytes at the section VA. sh_flags=SHF_ALLOC|SHF_WRITE(3) — readable + writable. Contributes to the R+W PT_LOAD's p_memsz but NOT its p_filesz. sh_addralign=8. virtualAddress=0 — the writer COMPUTES the `.bss` VA (last in the writable segment). D-LK4-DATA-PRODUCER.
sections.name: .bss

sections.$comment: Symbol table — even in ET_EXEC files, .symtab is conventional for debugger support. sh_type = SHT_SYMTAB (2); sh_addralign = 8; sh_entsize = 24 (Elf64_Sym).
sections.name: .symtab

sections.$comment: Symbol-name string table. sh_type = SHT_STRTAB (3); addrAlign = 1.
sections.name: .strtab

sections.$comment: Section-header string table. sh_type = SHT_STRTAB (3); addrAlign = 1.
sections.name: .shstrtab

relocations.$comment: Relocation kinds are declared so the linker engine's cross-reference unifier (plan 13 §2.6) accepts any relocation tag the assembler stamps. In ET_EXEC cycle 2, the walker rejects modules whose AssembledFunctions carry ANY relocation entries (cycle scope: self-contained only). Resolved at LK6 D-LK4-5/D-LK4-6 + plan 14 D-LK1-3.
relocations.name: R_X86_64_PC32
